title: $msgsrc
text: $msgsrc Return the name of the component instance that sent the message. $msgsrc Example: putmess "Message received from %%$msgsrc." Return Values String identifying the component instance that sent the message, if the receiveMessage trigger was activated by a postmessage statement. The string contains all the path information necessary to reply to the message. In addition, $msgid returns a non-empty string. If the receiveMessage trigger was not activated by postmessage , $msgid returns an empty string ( "" ). Use Allowed only in a receiveMessage trigger. Allowed in all component types. Example: Using $msgsrc The following code could appear in the receiveMessage trigger of a component: postmessage $msgsrc, "MSG001", "Reply to sender: message received." Copy To handle messages that are incorrectly addressed, the following code could appear in the receiveMessage trigger of the application. postmessage $msgsrc, "MSG001", "Reply to sender: instance addressed is unknown" Copy Related Links postmessage Send a message from one component instance to another. $msgdata Return the message data received from postmessage. $msgdst Return the name of the component instance to which a message was addressed. $msgid Return the identifier for a message received from postmessage. $msginfo Return the requested information about the latest message. trigger receiveMessage Reacts to asynchronous messages that come from outside the application.
